Let's talk make-up - Lips

The lipstickLip pencil
Module 4

Let's talk make-up - Lips

Lesson 1 - The lipstick

OBC lipstick

What it looks like:

Product overview

Lip care meets the desire for rich, bold and durable colour.

This vegan and dermatologically tested formula is smooth and creamy, with precise and even coverage and a velvety demi matte finish.

The deeply hydrating OBC lipstick is designed to penetrate the superficial layers of the skin and absorb water, creating a smoothing and volumizing effect that also helps enhance the skin's ability to retain moisture and improves skin firmness and elasticity.

Enriched with a mix of:

  • Wild mango butter, which is ideal for dry, damaged skin as it is rich in vitamins and minerals and offers protection against UV rays. Wild mango butter is also known to smooth wrinkles and restore skin's elasticity.
  • Hyaluronic spheres, which provide an enhanced plumping and hydrating effect.

What it does

  • Evens out the skin tone while still looking natural.
  • Adapts to the skin's natural colour for a seamless finish.
  • Gives the skin a soft glow with a velvety result.
  • Provides SPF15 protection from UV damage.
  • Hydrates, smooths, and improves skin texture.

Who is it for

Customers who:

  • Want very natural, "my skin but better" coverage.
  • Don't like wearing foundation.
  • Want skincare and make-up in one step.
  • Of all skin types.
  • Want light coverage with sun protection.

How to sell

  • "This adapts to your skin tone for a natural, even glow."
  • "It feels like skincare but gives your skin a polished look."
  • "Perfect if you don't like heavy foundation."
  • "It evens out your skin and includes SPF protection."

Top Tip

This is ideal for customers who want quick, easy make-up with skincare benefits.

When to recommend

Recommend this when a customer says they want light coverage, natural skin, or something quick and easy for everyday wear.

Test your knowledge

Learning Scenario (4 decisions): "I want bold colour... but I don't want dry lips"

Set the scene

A customer says:

C
"I want a rich lipstick that lasts, but my lips get dry easily. I also don't like sticky or messy colour."

Your goal: Recommend the right product and explain it simply and confidently.

Knowledge check

1. Decision 1: Identify the customer's main need. What is the customer mainly asking for?

2. Decision 2: Choose the right product. Which product best matches this need?

3. Decision 3: Explain what it will feel/look like. The customer asks: "What finish is it? Is it drying?" What is the best response?

4. Decision 4: Close the sale (solve the dry-lip concern). The customer says: "My lips always feel dry with lipstick." What is the best closing line?

Choose an answer, then check the question.

Lesson 2 - Lip pencil

OBC lip pencil

What it looks like:

Product overview

A creamy, soft and velvety consistency that glides over the lips and helps define the perfect lip contour.

A pencil that can also be used as a lipstick base, to enhance lipstick colour, and help it last longer.

Comes in 3 shades with a semi-matte finish, perfect for those who love the seductive look.

The formula's soft focus properties help to give the appearance of more volumized lips.

Enriched with a mix of:

  • Vitamin E, which is an antioxidant that protects the skin from damage caused by free radicals and pollution.
  • Vitamin E also moisturizes by reducing water loss which keeps the skin soft and elastic and also promotes skin repair and healing.
  • Offers UV protection, defending the skin from harmful ultraviolet rays.
  • Olus Oil, which is a moisturizing emollient, with anti oxidant properties, derived from grapeseed.
  • Forms a protective barrier on the skin, preventing water loss.
  • Ideal for dry and sensitive skin.
  • Helps defend the skin from environmental stress and premature aging.
  • Beeswax, a natural ingredient that softens and smooths the skin.
  • Emollient, film-forming, and protective properties.
  • Creates a protective barrier preventing moisture loss.
  • Silica microspheres, which are known for their ability to scatter light, creating a blurring effect that helps minimize the appearance of fine lines and imperfections.

What it does

  • Defines the lip shape for a clean, polished look.
  • Helps lipstick apply more evenly and last longer.
  • Creates the appearance of fuller, more volumised lips.
  • Smooths and softens the lips with a velvety finish.
  • Protects and conditions the lips while wearing it.

How to use it

As a lip liner:

  • Line the lips to define shape and prevent lipstick from feathering.

As a base:

  • Fill in the lips before applying lipstick to boost colour and wear time.

Who is it for

Customers who:

  • Want more defined lips.
  • Want their lipstick to last longer.
  • Like a seductive, semi-matte lip look.
  • Have dry or sensitive lips.
  • Want fuller-looking lips without gloss.

How to sell

  • "This defines your lips and helps your lipstick last longer."
  • "You can wear it alone or under lipstick as a base."
  • "It gives the look of fuller lips without feeling heavy."
  • "It's soft and comfortable, not dry."

Top Tip

Using it all over the lips helps lipstick look richer and last longer.

Test your knowledge

Learning Scenario (4 decisions): "My lipstick fades and bleeds"

Set the scene

A customer says:

C
"My lipstick never lasts. It fades quickly and sometimes it bleeds outside my lip line. I still want a soft, semi-matte look."

Your goal: Recommend the right product and explain how it helps in simple language.

Knowledge check

1. Decision 1: Identify the customer's main need. What problem are you solving?

2. Decision 2: Choose the right product. Which product best matches this need?

3. Decision 3: Explain how to use it (keep it simple). The customer asks: "How do I use it so my lipstick lasts?" What's the best advice?

4. Decision 4: Close the recommendation (sell line). Choose the best closing line:

Choose an answer, then check the question.
